Include every distinct reason and each action the user is told to take.

A well-known online publication focused on design and creative industries has curated and published a list highlighting 17 outstanding photography websites. Identify this publication and provide the full list of the 17 photography websites it selected, including a brief distinguishing feature or reason each site was recognized.

A prominent digital art and design publication released an editorial feature highlighting exactly 17 outstanding photography-focused websites, curated as exemplary sources of inspiration, portfolio design, and visual storytelling. Identify the publication responsible for this curated list and provide the full title of the feature. Additionally, explain the overall purpose of the article and specify how many websites are included in the roundup.

A well-known online publication focused on design, art, and digital creativity compiled and published a curated list highlighting 17 of the best photography websites. Identify this publication and then provide the full list of the 17 featured photography websites. For each of the 17 sites, briefly describe one distinctive characteristic (such as design style, navigation approach, portfolio presentation, or unique feature) that justifies its inclusion in the list.

A well-known online publication focused on design and digital creativity compiled and published a curated list highlighting 17 outstanding photography websites, emphasizing portfolio presentation and user experience. Identify the publication that created this list. Then, name all 17 photography websites featured and describe one distinctive design, technical, or stylistic feature noted for each site (for example, navigation style, layout approach, use of animation, or visual storytelling technique).

Identify the online design and creative industry publication that compiled a curated list of 17 of the best photography websites. Then, using that publication’s list, name all 17 featured photography websites and describe one specific characteristic, design feature, or reason for inclusion that is highlighted for each site.

A well-known online design and digital arts publication created a curated roundup highlighting what it considered the 17 best photography websites for inspiration. Identify the publication responsible for this roundup and list the names of all 17 photography websites it selected. Your answer should clearly state the publisher and provide the complete set of featured photography websites.

A well-known design and creative industry online magazine published a curated roundup highlighting 17 outstanding photography portfolio websites as exemplary for their visual design, user experience, and branding. Identify this publication and then list all 17 featured photography websites included in the roundup. For each website, provide the name of the photographer or studio and one specific design characteristic or distinguishing feature that the magazine highlights as a reason for its inclusion.

A well-known digital design and creative industry publication compiled a curated list highlighting what it described as the 17 best photography websites, showcasing standout portfolio examples from around the world. Identify all 17 photography websites included in that curated selection. For each website, provide: (1) the name of the photographer or studio behind it, (2) the country where the photographer or studio is primarily based, and (3) the main genre or specialization of photography featured (e.g., fashion, documentary, fine art, commercial, travel, etc.). Ensure your response covers every website in the curated list.

rounds.question: A well-known online design and creative industry magazine published a curated article highlighting 17 of the best photography websites. When attempting to browse this publication’s website, some users may encounter an automated security page that blocks access. Identify this magazine and подробно explain the full set of reasons the security system gives for suspecting a user is a bot, as well as all the steps and instructions it provides for regaining access. Include every distinct reason and each action the user is told to take.
checklist_width: Reason: User is moving through the website with super-human speed (power user behavior)
Reason: Cookies are disabled in the web browser
Reason: A third-party browser plugin (e.g., Ghostery or NoScript) is preventing JavaScript from running
Mention of additional information being available in a support article
Instruction: Ensure cookies are enabled
Instruction: Ensure JavaScript is enabled
Instruction: Reload the page to regain access
Message indicating the system is preparing access (e.g., 'Please stand by' / loading message)
checklist_depth: Creative Bloq + Identified as the online design/creative magazine that published the article featuring the 17 best photography websites
Correctly associates the anti-bot 'Pardon Our Interruption' security message with this publication’s website and explains its purpose
rounds.rationale: The question uses logical filtering (Deep) by requiring identification of the specific creative industry magazine known for publishing a curated list of 17 top photography websites, without naming it directly. It then requires broad aggregation (Wide) by demanding all listed reasons for bot suspicion and every recovery instruction from the security page, which are scattered across the blocking message. The respondent must both identify the correct publication and comprehensively extract multiple distinct details from its anti-bot notice.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Pardon Our Interruption As you were browsing something about your browser made us think you were a bot. There are a few reasons this might happen: Yo
rounds.answer_a:
The magazine that published a curated article on the 17 best photography websites is Creative Bloq [1]. When users encounter its automated security block, they are redirected to a page titled "Why have I been blocked?" which is a security feature implemented by Creative Bloq's parent company, Future PLC [2, 3].

This security system provides a comprehensive set of reasons for the block, suspecting the user is a bot or a threat. The full list of potential reasons includes:
*   The website's owner has established rules that block the user's specific request or IP address.
*   The user performed an action that triggered a security solution, such as submitting a malformed SQL command or unusual data.
*   The user's computer or IP address has been identified on a blocklist due to previously detected malicious activity [2, 4].

To regain access, the security page provides one primary instruction. The user is told they must contact the site owner for assistance. The page explicitly states that to resolve the issue, the user needs to provide two key pieces of information displayed on the screen: their IP address and the unique "Ray ID" associated with the blocking event. This information allows the site's administrators to investigate the specific security rule that was triggered and potentially whitelist the user's IP address [2, 4].

## References
[1] Creative Bloq. (2024). *The 17 best photography websites in 2024*. Retrieved from creativebloq.com.
[2] Future PLC. (n.d.). *Why have I been blocked?* Security Page. As observed on various Future PLC domains.
[3] G-Stat. (2024). *Creativebloq.com traffic*. Retrieved from g-stat.com.
[4] Various Authors. (n.d.). *Discussions on Cloudflare Community and Reddit regarding "Why have I been blocked?" page on Future PLC websites*. Retrieved from various online forums.
rounds.answer_b:
The magazine is **Creative Bloq**, a well-known online publication covering design, art, technology, and creative industries. Creative Bloq published a curated article titled "17 of the best photography websites" showcasing outstanding portfolio and photography sites for inspiration [1][2].

When some users attempt to access Creative Bloq's website, they may encounter an automated security verification page powered by Cloudflare or a similar service. This security page blocks access and provides specific reasons why the system suspects the user might be a bot, along with instructions for regaining access [2].

**Reasons the security system gives for triggering the block:**

1. **Submitting a certain word or phrase** — the user's input contains a term that triggers security filters.
2. **Submitting a SQL command** — the system detects what appears to be a database injection attempt in the user's request.
3. **Submitting malformed data** — the request contains improperly formatted or corrupted data that resembles automated or malicious activity [2].

**Steps and instructions provided to the user for regaining access:**

1. **Wait and try again** — the user is advised to attempt accessing the page again after a short period, as the block may be temporary.
2. **Contact the site owner** — the user is instructed to email the site owner or administrator to inform them that they were blocked, letting them know what they were doing when the block occurred.
3. **Include the Cloudflare Ray ID** — when contacting the site owner, the user must include the specific Cloudflare Ray ID displayed at the bottom of the security page, which helps identify the exact incident.
4. **Provide your current IP address** — the user should also share their IP address so the site administrator can investigate and whitelist them if appropriate [2].

These measures balance security with user accessibility, ensuring legitimate visitors can eventually regain access while protecting the site from automated threats.

## References

1. Creative Bloq, "17 of the best photography websites," https://www.creativebloq.com/photography/photo-websites-702702
2. Cloudflare Security Page encountered on Creative Bloq website, https://www.creativebloq.com
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both Agent A and Agent B correctly identify **Creative Bloq** as the magazine that published the article featuring 17 of the best photography websites. So both pass the core entity check.

However, both fail WIDTH-Completeness significantly. The required checklist includes specific anti-bot reasons (super-human speed, disabled cookies, JavaScript blocked by plugins like Ghostery/NoScript) and explicit recovery steps (enable cookies, enable JavaScript, reload the page, "Please stand by" message, reference to a support article). Neither agent mentions these. Instead, both provide generic Cloudflare-style explanations (SQL injection, malformed data, suspicious phrases, IP blocklists), which do not match the required security page details. They also fail to associate the correct "Pardon Our Interruption" style message or describe the cookie/JavaScript-based verification flow.

Because both responses miss nearly all required checklist items and substitute incorrect security explanations, they both fail the WIDE aggregation requirement in a substantial way. While Agent B has slightly better formatting and structure, this does not outweigh the major factual omissions shared by both.

Therefore, this is a LOW-QUALITY TIE: both agents identified the correct publication but failed to provide the specific reasons and recovery instructions required by the prompt.

rounds.answer_a:
The online publication Creative Boom curated a list titled "17 of the most outstanding photography websites" for their design, user experience, and visual impact [1]. The selection highlights portfolios that effectively blend creativity with functionality.

The 17 recognized websites and their distinguishing features are:
1.  **Tim Flach:** Known for conceptual animal portraits showcased on a clean, grid-based site.
2.  **Nick Balloon:** Features an engaging "photo of the week" and easy navigation.
3.  **Levon Biss:** Offers incredible zoom capabilities to explore his extreme macro photography.
4.  **Mike Kelley:** His "Airportraits" series is the centerpiece, set against a striking dark theme.
5.  **Pedro N'Kosi:** Employs a memorable and distinctive horizontal scroll feature.
6.  **Zena Holloway:** An underwater photographer whose site categorizes work into themes like "Water" and "Land."
7.  **Isabelle Wenzel:** Presents playful and surreal self-portraits in often contorted poses.
8.  **Elsa Bleda:** Showcases cinematic, atmospheric shots of urban environments at night.
9.  **Peter McKinnon:** A comprehensive hub for his brand, integrating photography, tutorials, and presets.
10. **Lauren Audra:** Uses a simple grid layout enhanced by cinematic GIFs and a compelling bio.
11. **Pari Dukovic:** A high-fashion feel accomplished through a dynamic horizontal scroll and oversized typography.
12. **Giles Clement:** A tintype photography specialist whose site has an authentic, historical aesthetic.
13. **Franziska & Tom:** A photography duo with a minimalist website that seamlessly combines their work.
14. **Guzelian:** A father-son team whose clean, simple website lets the images speak for themselves.
15. **Jack Davison:** Presents experimental black and white imagery in a simple, effective grid.
16. **Clayton Hauck:** Features a bold, cinematic style, with a website resembling a movie's opening credits.
17. **Cait Oppermann:** Utilizes a simple but impactful single-scrolling page with a thoughtful color scheme.

## References
[1] Creative Boom. (2024, February 13). *17 of the most outstanding photography websites to inspire you in 2024*. Retrieved from Creative Boom website.
rounds.answer_b:
**Webflow** is the well-known online design and creative industries publication that curated a list of 17 best photography websites [1]. Here are the 17 photography websites featured:

1. **Elena Iv-skaya** – Dreamlike, ethereal portrait photography with artistic post-processing.
2. **Felicia Simion** – Conceptual fine-art photography with moody, surreal aesthetics.
3. **Sean Mundy** – Minimalist, surreal conceptual portraits exploring isolation.
4. **Daniel Rueda & Anna Devís** – Playful, colorful architectural photography with creative human interaction.
5. **Jack Davison** – Raw, experimental portraiture with unconventional framing and lighting.
6. **Elizaveta Porodina** – High-fashion photography with vibrant colors and painterly quality.
7. **David William Baum** – Clean editorial and commercial photography portfolio.
8. **Joey L** – Cinematic portraiture and documentary work across global cultures.
9. **Platon** – Iconic close-up portraits of world leaders and influential figures.
10. **Jimmy Nelson** – Documentation of indigenous tribes and disappearing cultures worldwide.
11. **Brooke Shaden** – Dark, fairy-tale-inspired fine-art self-portraits with compositing.
12. **Tim Tadder** – High-energy commercial and sports photography with dynamic lighting.
13. **Erik Johansson** – Mind-bending surreal photo manipulations blending reality and fantasy.
14. **Levon Biss** – Extreme macro photography of insects with extraordinary detail.
15. **Vincent Urban** – Atmospheric travel and urban exploration photography.
16. **Sara Byrne** – Ethereal landscape photography capturing Ireland's natural beauty.
17. **Ami Vitale** – Award-winning photojournalism focusing on wildlife conservation and humanity [1].

Each site was recognized for distinctive visual storytelling, innovative web design showcasing their portfolios, and unique creative approaches that push photography boundaries across genres including fine art, commercial, editorial, and documentary work [1].

## References
[1] Webflow Blog, "17 of the Best Photography Websites," https://webflow.com/blog/photography-websites
rounds.judge_reasoning: Deep Logic check first: the ground truth specifies **Creative Bloq** as the design/creative publication that curated the list of 17 photography websites. Agent A identified **Creative Boom**, and Agent B identified **Webflow**—both incorrect, so both fail the core entity requirement. Width/Completeness: both agents do list 17 photography websites with brief distinguishing features, showing effort and reasonable formatting, but these details are anchored to the wrong publication and therefore do not satisfy the checklist. Because BOTH agents failed the main entity identification (DEEP) and thus the overall factual basis, this is a Low Quality Tie despite decent presentation and user-facing structure.

rounds.answer_a:
The digital art and design publication responsible for the curated list is **Creative Boom** [1]. The full title of the feature is **"17 of the most outstanding photography websites to inspire you in 2024"** [1].

The overall purpose of the article is to serve as a key source of inspiration for photographers and designers looking to create or enhance their own professional websites [1]. The publication argues that a well-designed online portfolio is an essential tool for attracting clients and that the quality of the website's design and user experience is as crucial as the photography itself [1]. The feature aims to showcase best practices in digital presentation, highlighting how exceptional layout, creative navigation, and unique features can elevate a photographer's work. By presenting a varied collection of sites—ranging from clean and minimalist to highly interactive and cinematic—the article provides a practical guide for creatives on how to effectively display their portfolios and build a memorable online brand.

The roundup, as explicitly stated in its title and throughout the article, includes a curated selection of exactly **17** photography-focused websites celebrated for their design and impact [1].

## References
[1] Creative Boom. (2024, February 13). *17 of the most outstanding photography websites to inspire you in 2024*. Retrieved from Creative Boom website.
rounds.answer_b:
**Webflow** is the prominent digital art and design publication responsible for this curated editorial feature. The full title of the feature is **"17 of the Best Photography Websites"**, published on the Webflow Blog [1].

**Overall Purpose of the Article:**

The article serves as an inspirational resource for photographers, web designers, and creative professionals looking to build or improve their online portfolios. It was curated to showcase exemplary photography websites that excel in three key areas: visual storytelling, innovative portfolio design, and creative inspiration [1]. The feature highlights how photographers across diverse genres—including fine art, commercial, editorial, documentary, and conceptual photography—leverage thoughtful web design to present their work in compelling and distinctive ways. By analyzing each selected website, the article provides readers with actionable insights into effective layout choices, navigation structures, color schemes, typography, and overall user experience that enhance the presentation of photographic work [1].

The roundup also emphasizes how a well-designed photography website can serve as more than just a gallery; it becomes an immersive experience that communicates the photographer's unique artistic vision and brand identity. The article encourages photographers to consider how design elements work in harmony with their images to create a cohesive online presence [1].

**Number of Websites Included:**

The roundup features exactly **17 photography websites**, each selected for its outstanding combination of photographic excellence and superior web design execution [1].

## References
[1] Webflow Blog, "17 of the Best Photography Websites," https://webflow.com/blog/photography-websites
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic check: The correct publication is Creative Bloq, and the article is titled "The 17 best photography websites." Agent A incorrectly identifies Creative Boom with a different title. Agent B incorrectly identifies Webflow as the publisher. Therefore, BOTH agents fail the core entity verification (DEEP failure).

Next, Width/Completeness: While both responses explain the article’s purpose (curating exemplary photography websites for inspiration and design reference) and correctly state that 17 websites are included, they both provide incorrect publication names and incorrect full titles. This results in WIDE checklist failures as well.

Since both agents hallucinate the main entity and title—critical components of the query—this constitutes a Low Quality Tie. Although both answers are well-formatted and reasonably comprehensive, accuracy is foundational, and both fail on the most important factual elements.

Here are the 17 featured photography websites with their distinctive characteristics:

6. **Elizaveta Porodina** – High-fashion work with bold, saturated colors and painterly post-processing [1].
7. **David William Baum** – Sleek, minimal portfolio layout ideal for editorial and commercial work [1].
8. **Joey L** – Cinematic portraiture with immersive full-screen gallery navigation [1].
9. **Platon** – Powerful close-up portraits of iconic figures with striking simplicity in design [1].
10. **Jimmy Nelson** – Grand-scale documentation of indigenous cultures with immersive scrolling presentation [1].
11. **Brooke Shaden** – Dark fairy-tale-inspired composites with narrative-driven portfolio structure [1].
12. **Tim Tadder** – High-energy commercial photography showcased through dynamic, bold layouts [1].
13. **Erik Johansson** – Surreal photo manipulations with interactive behind-the-scenes process breakdowns [1].
14. **Levon Biss** – Extreme macro insect photography presented with extraordinary zoomable detail [1].
15. **Vincent Urban** – Atmospheric urban exploration photography with moody, cinematic tones [1].
16. **Sara Byrne** – Ethereal Irish landscapes with serene, nature-focused minimalist design [1].
17. **Ami Vitale** – Award-winning photojournalism combining conservation storytelling with editorial web design [1].

Each site was selected for exceptional visual storytelling paired with innovative web design that elevates portfolio presentation [1].

## References
[1] Webflow Blog, "17 of the Best Photography Websites," https://webflow.com/blog/photography-websites

However, since the foundational publication is wrong in both cases, the lists are likely drawn from entirely different articles and cannot be trusted as accurate to the prompt.

User Experience & Formatting: Both responses are well-formatted, clearly structured, and easy to scan. Agent A is slightly cleaner in presentation, while Agent B uses stronger header emphasis. However, these are soft advantages and do not compensate for the core factual error.

Because both agents fail the primary entity identification (DEEP logic), this is a LOW QUALITY TIE.

The online design and creative industry publication that compiled the list is **Creative Boom** [1]. Its feature highlights 17 of the best photography websites for their inspiring design and user experience.

The 17 featured websites and their specific characteristics are:

17. **Cait Oppermann:** Uses an impactful single-scrolling page defined by a thoughtful color scheme.

rounds.question: A well-known digital design and creative industry publication compiled a curated list highlighting what it described as the 17 best photography websites, showcasing standout portfolio examples from around the world. Identify all 17 photography websites included in that curated selection. For each website, provide: (1) the name of the photographer or studio behind it, (2) the country where the photographer or studio is primarily based, and (3) the main genre or specialization of photography featured (e.g., fashion, documentary, fine art, commercial, travel, etc.). Ensure your response covers every website in the curated list.
checklist_width: Name of the photographer or studio for each of the 17 websites
Country of origin/base for each of the 17 photographers or studios
Primary photography genre or specialization for each of the 17 websites
Complete coverage of all 17 featured websites in the curated selection
checklist_depth: Correct identification of all 17 photography websites included in the specific curated 'best photography websites' list published by the major digital design publication
Implicit proof that each named website appears in that exact curated list (not from a different ranking or article)
No substitutions from other rankings; entities must match the original 17 selected in the referenced publication
rounds.rationale: The question masks the source by referring only to a well-known design publication and its curated selection, without naming the article or outlet (Deep). It requires identifying the exact 17 websites from that specific list and aggregating multiple factual attributes (creator, country, specialization) for each one, ensuring broad cross-verification across all entries (Wide).

rounds.answer_a:
The 17 photography websites included in the curated selection by the digital design publication Creative Boom represent a global array of talent and genres. The photographers, their primary countries of operation, and their specializations are as follows [1]:

1.  **Tim Flach** (United Kingdom): A fine art photographer celebrated for conceptualized and highly stylized portraits of animals.
2.  **Nick Balloon** (United Kingdom): A travel and documentary photographer with a significant portfolio focused on Latin America.
3.  **Levon Biss** (United Kingdom): Specializes in extreme macro photography, particularly of insects, alongside dynamic sports and portrait work.
4.  **Mike Kelley** (United States): An architectural and aviation photographer acclaimed for his "Airportraits" series compositing air traffic.
5.  **Pedro N’Kosi** (United Kingdom): A London-based photographer focused on evocative fashion and portraiture with a distinctively moody aesthetic.
6.  **Zena Holloway** (United Kingdom): A pioneering underwater photographer creating ethereal fine art and commercial imagery.
7.  **Isabelle Wenzel** (Netherlands): A fine art and performance artist who uses her body to create surreal, acrobatic self-portraits.
8.  **Elsa Bleda** (South Africa): Renowned for cinematic and atmospheric fine art photography capturing urban environments at night.
9.  **Peter McKinnon** (Canada): A prominent photographer and content creator specializing in landscape, adventure, and lifestyle genres.
10. **Lauren Audra** (United States): A Brooklyn-based artist whose work centers on intimate portrait and lifestyle photography.
11. **Pari Dukovic** (United States): A New York-based photographer recognized for dynamic celebrity, portrait, and high-fashion work.
12. **Giles Clement** (United States): Specializes in historical portraiture using 19th-century wet plate collodion tintype and ambrotype processes.
13. **Franziska & Tom** (Germany): A Berlin-based photography duo focused on authentic people, lifestyle, and commercial imagery.
14. **Guzelian** (United Kingdom): A father-son photojournalism agency known for news and editorial coverage in the north of England.
15. **Jack Davison** (United Kingdom): An experimental photographer whose work, mainly in black and white, blurs the lines between fine art and portraiture.
16. **Clayton Hauck** (United States): A Chicago-based commercial photographer and director known for a bold, cinematic advertising style.
17. **Cait Oppermann** (United States): A documentary, travel, and portrait photographer with a focus on themes of community and sport.

## References
[1] Creative Boom. (2024, February 13). *17 of the most outstanding photography websites to inspire you in 2024*. Retrieved from Creative Boom website.
